title: Global Geoparks Network (GGN) & UNESCO Global Geoparks
focus: UNESCO Global Geoparks framework, GGN membership criteria, and India's current status.
rationale: A distinct topic on UNESCO's geological conservation framework. It is highly testable for statement-based questions regarding UGGP criteria, reassessment periods, and India's lack of recognized sites.
sectionExcerpt: GGN is a non-profit International Association founded under UNESCO, which establishes ethical standards to be adopted by Global Geoparks. Introduced in 2015 as a key component of the International Geosciences and Geoparks Programme (IGGP). Geoparks: Single, unified geographical areas where landscapes of international geological significance are managed with a holistic concept... UGGP status is not permanent; it is reassessed every four years. Mandatory Networking: Membership of the GGN is obligatory for UGGPs. Current Status: 229 UNESCO Global Geoparks in 50 countries (none in India).
evidenceBullets: GGN is a non-profit International Association under UNESCO that establishes ethical standards for Global Geoparks.
UNESCO Global Geoparks (UGGP) were introduced in 2015 under the International Geosciences and Geoparks Programme (IGGP).
UGGP status is not permanent and must be reassessed every four years.
Membership in the Global Geoparks Network (GGN) is obligatory for all UGGPs.
There are currently 229 UNESCO Global Geoparks globally, but none are located in India.

title: Polymetallic Sulphides & International Seabed Authority (ISA)
focus: India's deep-sea exploration contracts, characteristics of polymetallic sulphides/nodules, and the mandate/initiatives of the ISA.
rationale: A high-yield UPSC topic combining geography (ocean ridges), economy (marine resources), and international relations (ISA, UNCLOS). Excellent for mapping questions (Carlsberg Ridge, Afanasy-Nikitin) and institutional questions (ISA).
sectionExcerpt: India signed a 15-year exploration contract for polymetallic sulphides (PMS) with International Seabed Authority (ISA) in Carlsberg Ridge. This is the first licence granted globally for exploring polymetallic sulphur nodules in the Carlsberg Ridge... Other Indian Contracts: Polymetallic Nodules in the Central Indian Ocean Basin and PMS in the Indian Ocean Ridge. Yet to be approved: Afanasy-Nikitin Sea (ANS) mount... claimed by Sri Lanka. Polymetallic sulphides are rich sources of metals such as copper, zinc, gold, and silver... ISA Location: Kingston, Jamaica. Established in 1994 under UNCLOS. Initiatives: Deep-Sea Biobank Initiative (DBI) 2025, AREA2030, Sustainable Seabed Knowledge Initiative (SSKI).
evidenceBullets: India signed a 15-year contract with the ISA for exploring polymetallic sulphides in the Carlsberg Ridge, located in the Arabian Sea.
India holds other exploration contracts for Polymetallic Nodules in the Central Indian Ocean Basin and PMS in the Indian Ocean Ridge.
India's exploration approval for the Afanasy-Nikitin Sea (ANS) mount is pending, as the territory is claimed by Sri Lanka.
Polymetallic sulphides are rich in copper, zinc, gold, and silver, and form where hot, mineral-rich fluids from the Earth's mantle enter the ocean.
The International Seabed Authority (ISA) is an autonomous organization established in 1994 under UNCLOS, headquartered in Kingston, Jamaica.
ISA initiatives include the Deep-Sea Biobank Initiative (DBI) 2025, AREA2030, and the Sustainable Seabed Knowledge Initiative (SSKI).

title: Bharat Forecast System (BFS) & Mission Mausam
focus: India's indigenous high-resolution weather forecast system, its technical features, and related meteorological initiatives.
rationale: Important science and technology / geography topic focusing on indigenous forecasting capabilities, High-Performance Computing (HPC) systems, and Doppler radar applications.
sectionExcerpt: Ministry of Earth Sciences unveiled BFS, world's first indigenously developed high-resolution weather forecast systems. Developed by: Indian Institute of Tropical Meteorology (IITM), Pune... Enhances India's weather prediction resolution from 12 km to 6 km... Integrates real-time inputs from 40 Doppler Weather Radar. Positions India ahead of countries like US, UK, and EU by achieving 6 km resolution. Powered by High-Performance Computing (HPC) systems - Arka (IITM Pune) and Arunika (National Centre for Medium Range Weather Forecasting, Delhi). Mission Mausam: Launched in 2024 by Ministry of Earth Sciences (MoES).
evidenceBullets: Bharat Forecast System (BFS) is the world's first indigenously developed high-resolution weather forecast system, created by IITM, Pune.
BFS improves India's weather prediction resolution from 12 km to 6 km, which is ahead of global models that operate at 9-14 km.
The system integrates real-time data from 40 Doppler Weather Radars, which use the Doppler effect to gather velocity data from particles.
BFS is powered by High-Performance Computing (HPC) systems named Arka (IITM Pune) and Arunika (NCMRWF, Delhi).
Mission Mausam was launched in 2024 by the Ministry of Earth Sciences to improve weather and climate services.
